perturbation.original_text: 3.4 ACCEPTANCE OF MILESTONE DELIVERABLE; CHANGE REQUESTS Client will notify Developer in writing of any failure of a Milestone Deliverable to comply with the Specifications, or of any other objections, corrections, changes or amendments required (a Change Request), within ten (10) days of such Milestone's Acceptance Test Date. Any Change Request shall be sufficient to identify, with clarity, any objection, correction, change or amendment to such Milestone Deliverable. In the absence of a Change Request from Client within the time periods detailed herein, the Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.4 ACCEPTANCE OF MILESTONE DELIVERABLE; CHANGE REQUESTS Client will notify Developer in writing of any failure of a Milestone Deliverable to comply with the Specifications, or of any other objections, corrections, changes or amendments required (a Change Request), within five (5) days of such Milestone's Acceptance Test Date. Any Change Request shall be sufficient to identify, with clarity, any objection, correction, change or amendment to such Milestone Deliverable. In the absence of a Change Request from Client within the time periods detailed herein, the Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the notification deadline for Milestone Deliverable failures from ten (10) days to five (5) days. This creates conflict because Section 3.7, which concerns final deliverable testing, still references a ten-day window for change requests related to initial acceptance test results. Thus, the change in 3.4 creates ambiguity about the applicable timeframe for change requests throughout the project lifecycle. This could lead to disputes about whether a change request was submitted within the correct timeframe.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7 FINAL DELIVERABLE AND SUBJECT PROGRAM TESTING Notwithstanding anything contained herein, upon completion of the Final Deliverable set out in the Specifications thereto, Client will perform Acceptance Test on the Subject Program within fifteen (15) calendar days from the Acceptance Test Date in order to determine whether the Subject Program satisfies the acceptance criteria and operates with internal consistency. If the completed Subject Program does not satisfy the Client's Acceptance Tests and Client provides Developer a Change Request concerning the same, Developer will have fifteen (15) calendar days from the receipt of such Change Request to correct the deficiencies errors, corrections, modifications, bug-fixes or changes. Client shall then have ten (10) additional days to inspect, test and reevaluate the completed and modified Subject Program or Final Deliverable. If the Subject Program or Final Deliverable still does not satisfy the Client's acceptance criteria and/or the Acceptance Tests, Client shall have the option of either: (a) repeating the procedure set forth above; or (b) terminating this Agreement pursuant to Section 12 of this Agreement . If the Client does not notify the Developer of any further failures, objections, changes, or other defects, or bugs of or in the Subject Program via a Change Request, Client will be deemed to have accepted the Subject Program.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3.4 shortens the timeframe for submitting change requests from ten (10) days to five (5) days. Section 3.7, however, explicitly states that the Client has ten (10) additional days to inspect, test, and reevaluate the completed and modified Subject Program or Final Deliverable after the Developer corrects deficiencies identified in the initial acceptance test. The altered timeframe in 3.4 directly contradicts the ten-day period referenced in 3.7, creating an inconsistency and uncertainty regarding the correct deadline for change requests related to final deliverables.

perturbation.original_text: 12.2 NOTICE A notice required or permitted under this Agreement will be deemed given if in writing, and delivered by a Party in person, one (1) business day after being sent via overnight carrier, or three (3) business days after being sent by certified mail return receipt requested to the address set forth below, or such other address as may be supplied by either Party subsequently: If to Developer: SEATTLE SOFTWARE DEVELOPERS, INC 4-102ND AvenueNE, Suite 300 Bellevue, Washington 98004 IF to attorney: Copy to: Perkins Coie 1201 third avenue, Seattle, Washington 98101 Attn: LUCAS S. MICHELS, ESQ. If to Client: Pelican Delivers Inc. Dave Comeau 5452 Pineridge Drive Bremerton, WA 983 360-731-6611
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2 NOTICE A notice required or permitted under this Agreement will be deemed given if in writing, and delivered by a Party in person, two (2) business day after being sent via overnight carrier, or five (5) business days after being sent by certified mail return receipt requested to the address set forth below, or such other address as may be supplied by either Party subsequently: If to Developer: SEATTLE SOFTWARE DEVELOPERS, INC 4-102ND AvenueNE, Suite 300 Bellevue, Washington 98004 IF to attorney: Copy to: Perkins Coie 1201 third avenue, Seattle, Washington 98101 Attn: LUCAS S. MICHELS, ESQ. If to Client: Pelican Delivers Inc. Dave Comeau 5452 Pineridge Drive Bremerton, WA 983 360-731-6611
perturbation.explanation: This alteration introduces a contradiction concerning the effectiveness of contractual notices. By modifying the delivery timeframes for notices (overnight carrier from one to two business days and certified mail from three to five business days), Section 12.2 now conflicts with the time-sensitive deadlines outlined in other sections of the agreement, specifically Sections 3.4 and 3.5 which deal with acceptance and rejection of deliverables as well as change request deadlines. The extended notice periods could potentially cause a missed deadline in either one of the sections, since the party might argue the timelines are ambiguous. This ambiguity undermines the enforceability of those deadlines.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4 ACCEPTANCE OF MILESTONE DELIVERABLE; CHANGE REQUESTS Client will notify Developer in writing of any failure of a Milestone Deliverable to comply with the Specifications, or of any other objections, corrections, changes or amendments required (a Change Request), within five (5) days of such Milestone's Acceptance Test Date. Any Change Request shall be sufficient to identify, with clarity, any objection, correction, change or amendment to such Milestone Deliverable. In the absence of a Change Request from Client within the time periods detailed herein, the Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice periods in Section 12.2, increasing the time for notices delivered by overnight carrier and certified mail. This directly conflicts with Section 3.4, which requires the Client to notify the Developer of any issues with a Milestone Deliverable within five days of the Acceptance Test Date. The extended notice periods introduced in Section 12.2 could cause a party to miss the deadline in Section 3.4, thus creating a contradiction regarding enforceable deadlines and creating uncertainty, and thus this is a strong in-text contradiction.
